metadata.title: A Battery Can Be Full and Still Crash Your Device

beats.narration_text: Bonjour, Liam. This is Liam, in for Bear. The coin cell had most of its charge left, and the device browned out and rebooted anyway.

props.command: Why would a device crash mid-inference if the battery is still mostly full?
output: Internal resistance caps current; inference burst drags voltage below reset threshold

beats.narration_text: This annotated current trace is from the book — every phase of one duty cycle: sleep floor, wake-up spike, inference peak, settle, back to sleep.
The coin cell reads 80 percent charge. The battery meter looks fine. Then the device runs an inference, draws a burst of current, and resets. The charge level had nothing to do with it.

The problem is internal resistance. A CR2032 has roughly 35 ohms internal resistance. At 10 milliamps inference burst: 3 volts minus 10 milliamps times 35 ohms equals 2.65 volts — below the 2.7-volt reset threshold. Voltage sags, triggers reset. The battery was fine. The instantaneous voltage was not.

Power analysis has two completely different questions. How long will it run — that is energy, and you use average current. Will it run at all — that is peak instantaneous current against the supply's ability to deliver it. Average told you six months. Peak told you it would crash.

Your turn. Paste this into Claude: I have a CR2032 coin cell — 3 volts nominal, 35 ohms internal resistance. My MCU draws peak-mA milliamps during inference bursts and sleep-µA microamps otherwise. Compute: one — terminal voltage during a burst; two — whether it crosses my 2.7-volt reset threshold; three — what bulk capacitance would keep voltage above reset during a duration-ms millisecond burst. Plug in your peak current and burst duration and let Claude flag whether your design survives.

props.command: I have a CR2032 coin cell (3V nominal, ~35 Ω internal resistance). My MCU draws [peak_mA] mA during inference bursts and [sleep_uA] µA otherwise. Compute: (1) terminal voltage during a burst, (2) whether it crosses my 2.7V reset threshold, (3) what bulk capacitance would keep voltage above reset during a [duration_ms] ms burst.

props.title: A Battery Can Be Full and Still Crash Your Device
props.subline: embedded ai · liam, in for bear
